summary: Good 1978 show with solid Jack Straw and Cold Rain openings; the audience recording is a bit stark but improves with added reverb, though Jerry’s vocals sound thin and Round & Round falls short.
review: The performance is generally enjoyable – highlights include the opening Jack Straw and Cold Rain & Snow, and a respectable Black Peter.  The fan‑recorded tape sounds a little harsh out of the box, but a touch of reverb and extra bass make it more listenable; Jerry’s voice is marginally thin and the Round & Round jam disappoints for some listeners.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviewers gave 4‑star ratings, praising the show but noting vocal and recording flaws; weighting show (70%) and recording (30%) yields a mid‑high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as 'a bit stark' with room‑tone issues; adding reverb and extra bass improves clarity and warmth.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong openings and solid middle set, but the ending jam (Round & Round) is viewed as a letdown.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals are marginally thin; barely holding it together tonally.
